1: habbōṭĕḥîm bayhwāh kĕhar-ṣiyyôn lōʾ-yimmôṭ lĕʿôlām yēšēb — "those who trust in the LORD are as firm as Mount Zion — unmovable, abiding forever" — the simile places divine trust on par with the geological permanence of Zion. bāṭaḥ (to trust, be confident) is the covenant-confidence word throughout the Psalter. lōʾ-yimmôṭ (will not be shaken/moved, Niphal of māṭaṭ) — cf. Ps 112:6 (the righteous will not be moved). Mount Zion's permanence is YHWH's own choice (Ps 132:13-14) — those who trust YHWH share in the stability he has chosen for his dwelling place.
2: yĕrûšālāim hārîm sābîb lāh wayhwāh sābîb lĕʿammô mēʿattāh wĕʿad-ʿôlām — "as the mountains encircle Jerusalem, so the LORD encircles his people, from now and forever" — the geographic simile is exact: Jerusalem sits in a basin surrounded by higher ridges (Mount of Olives, Mount Scopus, Mount Zion, Gihon valley). The surrounding mountains are the city's natural fortification. YHWH's encirclement of his people (sābîb, around, on all sides) is at least as complete as the mountains around Jerusalem — and permanent in a way mountains are not.
3: kî lōʾ-yānûaḥ šēbeṭ hārāšāʿ ʿal gôral haṣṣaddîqîm lĕmaʿan lōʾ-yišlĕḥû haṣṣaddîqîm bĕʿawlāh yĕdêhem — "for the scepter of the wicked will not rest over the allotted territory of the righteous, lest the righteous put out their hands to do wrong" — šēbeṭ hārāšāʿ (the rod/scepter of the wicked) — political oppression by wicked rulers. gôral haṣṣaddîqîm (the allotment/lot of the righteous) — the land inheritance (gôral, the lot cast for tribal territories, Josh 14-19). The theological argument: prolonged oppression risks moral corruption of the oppressed — YHWH removes the oppressor not merely for justice but to protect the righteous from the temptation to adopt wicked means.
4: hêṭîbāh yhwh laṭṭôbîm ûlĕyišārîm bĕlibbôtām — "do good, O LORD, to those who are good, and to those who are upright in their hearts" — the prayer hêṭîbāh (do good, Hiphil imperative of yāṭab) — an appeal for positive divine action following the negative protection of v3. laṭṭôbîm (to those who are good) and lĕyišārîm bĕlibbôtām (to those who are upright in their hearts) — the internal qualifier "in their hearts" distinguishes genuine uprightness from external conformity.
5: wĕhammaṭṭîm ʿaqalqallôtām yôlîkēm yhwh ʾet-pōʿălê hāʾāwen šālôm ʿal-yiśrāʾēl — "but as for those who swerve onto crooked paths, the LORD will banish them along with those who do evil. Peace be upon Israel!" — hammaṭṭîm ʿaqalqallôtām (those who turn to their crooked ways) — ʿaqalqal (twisted, crooked, devious) — the word is an intensive form suggesting deeply bent, tortuous paths. pōʿălê hāʾāwen (workers of wickedness/iniquity) — the same phrase as Ps 6:8 and many other psalms. The closing šālôm ʿal-yiśrāʾēl (peace upon Israel) is the standard liturgical close of many Psalms of Ascents (cf. Ps 128:6; 131:3).
